Home › Services › Income Tax Filing

Income Tax Return (ITR) Filing in Mumbai

Income tax return (ITR) filing is the annual reporting of income and tax to the Income Tax Department. We prepare and file the correct ITR form for salaried individuals, professionals, and businesses in Mumbai.

For individuals not subject to audit, the ITR due date is generally 31 July of the assessment year; for taxpayers requiring a tax audit it is generally 31 October — as per the Income Tax Act, 1961. Choosing the right form (ITR-1 to ITR-7) and tax regime materially affects the outcome.

What is the process?

  1. Determine the applicable ITR form and tax regime (old vs new).
  2. Collect income, deduction, and TDS details (Form 16, AIS, 26AS).
  3. Compute total income, deductions, and tax; reconcile with TDS.
  4. File the return and assist with e-verification and any notices.

What documents are required?

  • PAN and Aadhaar
  • Form 16 / salary details
  • Bank interest, capital gains, other income
  • Investment and deduction proofs (80C, 80D, etc.)
  • Form 26AS and Annual Information Statement (AIS)
Typical timelineMost straightforward returns are filed within 2–5 working days of receiving complete information; complex cases take longer.
FeesFees vary by return type and complexity. Contact us for a quote.

Source: Income Tax Act, 1961 and CBDT — incometax.gov.in. Due dates may be extended by official notification; we confirm current dates at the time of filing.

Request a quote

Frequently asked questions

What is the ITR filing due date?
For individuals not requiring a tax audit, the due date is generally 31 July of the assessment year; for audit cases it is generally 31 October, as per the Income Tax Act, 1961. Dates can be extended by CBDT notification.
Which ITR form should I use?
It depends on your income sources — for example, ITR-1 (Sahaj) suits most salaried individuals with income up to a limit, while business and capital-gains income use other forms. We determine the correct form for you.
Old regime or new regime — which is better?
It depends on your deductions and income. We compute the tax under both and file under the one that results in lower tax for your situation.
What is the official income tax portal?
The official e-filing portal is incometax.gov.in, operated by the Income Tax Department.
